title: Lomelosia reuteriana
taxonomy: Lomelosia reuteriana is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Dipsacaceae. It is a member of the genus Lomelosia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as teasels. The genus Lomelosia is part of the order Dipsacales, a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as teasel family. The order Dipsacales is further classified under the clade Asterids, a large group of flowering plants that also includes families like Campanulaceae and Caprifoliaceae. The clade Asterids is part of the larger clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants.

intro: Lomelosia reuteriana, also known as Reuter's Lomelosia or Reuter's Scrophularia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, Lomelosia reuteriana is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lomelosia reuteriana is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
